english: Heinrich's particle of reality. This is how things are, no drama needed. It is just so.
Heinrich says: 'It is just the way it is.'
The table is full. Roman pasta, Hildi's Kartoffelgratin, Lukas's cake, Mia's wine. Everyone is here. Every register of German in one room.
Sit at the table with everyone
Practice all B2 constructs through natural conversation
Experience the full range of German registers in one room
The table is full. Rosemary pasta from Verumius's mother's recipe. Hildi's potato gratin. Lukas's cake. Mia's wine. The room is warm with candles and conversation.
To Verumius! Two years ago he couldn't speak a word of German. Now he cooks for us and invites us over.
And he once beat me at the word chain game. Stage fright. I will just never forget that.
I remember the day he said 'der Milch' in the REWE supermarket. I nearly had a heart attack.
And when he ordered 'one rye bread, please' for the first time. Without a mistake. That is when I knew: he is staying.
His first essay was three sentences long. With eleven mistakes. The last essay passed the exam.
Each of you taught me something. Not just words. Patience. Courage. Serenity. I am grateful.
appreciation, esteem

A: I want to express my appreciation.
B: For what exactly?
A: For your patience with me.
B: That goes without saying.
spirit of reconciliation, forgiveness

A: Hildi shows reconciliation with the past.
B: What do you mean?
A: She talks about Werner without pain.
B: That is true maturity.
commitment, reliability

A: Commitment is important to me.
B: What does that mean for you?
A: When I say something, I do it.
B: I really appreciate that.
retrospective, a look back

A: In retrospect it was the best decision.
B: Which decision?
A: Moving to Berlin.
B: Yes, in retrospect that is true.
departure, new beginning

A: Today is a day of new beginnings.
B: Where does the journey go?
A: Into a new chapter.
B: Then let's go!
to value, to hold in esteem

A: I value your friendship.
B: We value you too.
A: That means a lot to me.
B: To us too.
to look back, to retrospect

A: When I look back, everything was right.
B: Even the hard times?
A: Especially the hard times.
B: That is wisdom.
to process, to work through

A: Have you processed everything?
B: Yes, it took me a long time.
A: That is normal.
B: Now I feel lighter.
to slow down, to decelerate

A: I need to slow down my life.
B: How do you want to do that?
A: Work less, enjoy more.
B: That sounds like a good plan.
to content oneself, to settle for

A: Can you be content with that?
B: Yes, I am content with it.
A: No regrets?
B: No. Contentment is a decision.
conciliatory, forgiving

A: He was very conciliatory.
B: Despite everything that happened?
A: Yes. A forgiving look, a handshake.
B: That takes strength.
empathetic, sensitive

A: Mia is really empathetic.
B: Yes, she notices right away how you are doing.
A: That is a rare ability.
B: The best friends are empathetic.
gentle, careful, cautious

A: He spoke gently with her.
B: About Werner?
A: Yes. One must be careful.
B: Some topics need delicacy.
moving, touching

A: Heinrich's gift was so moving.
B: Twenty-year-old sourdough starter.
A: The most personal gift of all.
B: I almost cried.
Count the distinct B2 features in the sentence: one is a small word expressing resignation or acceptance, the other is a subordinate clause type that describes the method or means of doing something.
Deeply moving. What stirs your emotions. A twenty-year-old sourdough starter from a baker's life.
Heinrich's gift was deeply moving.
Two particles: the first signals personal probability rather than certainty, the second invites the listener to share what is already known. Neither is a particle of resignation or contradiction.
Hildi uses his first name. KD arrives late. The tablet comes out. Every character arc reaches its resolution.
Experience the emotional climax of the story
See all character arcs resolve
Practice all constructs in their most natural form
The table goes quiet for a moment. Hildi reaches for her wine glass. She looks at Verumius. Not Herr Verumius. Not 'the new one.' She looks at him the way you look at family.
Verumius... the wine is really good.

His first name. In front of everyone. She offered him 'du' months ago. But saying his name here, at this table, in front of these people: that is the last wall falling. Mia grins. Lukas raises his glass. Yilmaz smiles. Heinrich nods.
He does not speak. He smiles. That is enough.
The doorbell. KD. Slightly out of breath. A small cactus in one hand. An apology on his face.
Sorry! Paperwork. Always paperwork. I brought a cactus. For the collection.
Later. Hildi opens a tablet. The tablet Verumius taught her to use. She shows photos of Werner. Her late husband. The garden they built together. The cats. 'That is Werner. He would have liked you.'
Nobody speaks for a moment. Then Heinrich puts his hand on Hildi's shoulder. Mia pours her more wine. The moment passes gently, like a cloud.
surprise

A: What a surprise!
B: Didn't you expect that?
A: No, not at all.
B: The best surprises come unexpectedly.
deep sigh of relief

A: KD let out a deep sigh.
B: Why?
A: He made it just in time.
B: Typical KD. Always at the last minute.
inner self, inner being

A: Deep inside he had known it for a long time.
B: Known what exactly?
A: That Berlin is his home.
B: Some things you just feel.
leisure, contemplation time

A: Tonight we finally have leisure.
B: No hurry, no appointments.
A: Just being here.
B: That is the greatest luxury.
dream

A: Was this always your dream?
B: Yes, since childhood.
A: And now the dream is reality.
B: Sometimes I can hardly believe it myself.
to become reconciled, to make peace

A: Have you made peace with the past?
B: Yes, finally.
A: That takes time.
B: But it is worth it.
to allow, to permit, to let in

A: One must allow feelings.
B: That is not always easy.
A: No, but necessary.
B: Allowing is sometimes the greatest courage.
to grow together

A: This group has grown together.
B: Over the course of two years.
A: Strangers became friends.
B: That does not happen often.
to rest completely

A: Get some rest.
B: Yes, I am really tired.
A: You have earned it.
B: Two years of hard work.
to breathe a sigh of relief

A: Everyone breathed a sigh of relief when KD arrived.
B: Why?
A: Because now everyone was really here.
B: The table was complete.
happy, fortunate

A: Are you happy?
B: Yes. Truly happy for the first time.
A: You can see it on your face.
B: Thanks. I feel it too.
priceless, invaluable

A: This evening is priceless.
B: Yes, you cannot buy something like this.
A: True friendship is priceless.
B: The most valuable thing there is.
relaxed, informal, natural

A: The atmosphere was totally relaxed.
B: Yes, nobody was pretending.
A: Everyone was simply themselves.
B: That is how it should be.
confident, optimistic

A: I look to the future with confidence.
B: What comes next?
A: I don't know yet.
B: But you are ready.
There is a shift in how Hildi addresses Verumius here. The narrator describes this as the last wall falling. What has changed in how she refers to him?
A separable verb describing organic bonds forming over time. Strangers who have slowly become unified, like trees whose roots meet underground.
This group has grown together over two years.
Three gaps: an adjective for something money cannot buy, a connector introducing HOW belonging is created, and a particle of quiet acceptance. Think about what friendship is worth.
KD arriving late is entirely predictable. This particle captures quiet acceptance of something unchangeable, without drama or argument.
The final assessment. Every B2 construct. Every register. Every skill. Prove everything you have learned across 36 units.
Prove mastery of all B2 constructs
Switch between formal and informal registers
Demonstrate full German competence
to proceed, to betake oneself (very formal)

A: Please proceed to the exit.
B: Where is the exit?
A: At the end of the hallway, on the right.
B: Thank you.
to take from, to gather/infer (formal)

A: From the text you can gather that...
B: Yes, I gathered that from the report.
A: Good. Please also extract the numbers.
B: Understood.
to be located (formal)

A: Where is the museum located?
B: It is located in the city center.
A: And the train station?
B: That is located right across from it.
to behold, to catch sight of (literary)

A: He beheld the city for the first time.
B: How was the moment?
A: Overwhelming. He could hardly believe it.
B: Berlin has that effect.
almost, nearly (slightly formal)

A: I almost gave up.
B: But you persevered.
A: Yes, it was almost too much.
B: Almost is not quite.
however (formal)

A: The exam was hard, however he passed.
B: Despite all the difficulties?
A: Yes. He had prepared well, however.
B: Preparation is everything.
furthermore, moreover (very formal)

A: Furthermore, it should be noted that...
B: What else?
A: Furthermore, the following rules apply.
B: Understood. Is there anything else?
immediately, at once (literary)

A: He came at once.
B: Without hesitation?
A: Yes, immediately. Not a second's delay.
B: That is what you call determination.
knowledge of human nature, people skills

A: Yilmaz has great people skills.
B: Where does that come from?
A: From years of experience with students.
B: You learn to read people.
self-actualization

A: Language is a path to self-actualization.
B: In what way?
A: You become yourself in a new language.
B: Verumius is the best example.
willpower

A: Without willpower he would have given up.
B: True. It was often hard.
A: But his willpower was stronger.
B: The engine behind everything.
sense of belonging together

A: At this table you can feel a sense of belonging.
B: Even though everyone is so different?
A: Precisely because of that.
B: Belonging does not require sameness.
unconditional

A: True friendship is unconditional.
B: Without ifs and buts?
A: Exactly. You accept the other person completely.
B: That is rare and valuable.
for each other

A: They are there for each other.
B: In good times and bad?
A: Yes, always for each other.
B: That is the most important thing.
'Sich begeben' is what you hear on a train. 'Geh raus' is what Lukas would say. Same meaning, different worlds.
Which connector answers the question HOW belonging is created? It introduces the method or means by which something happens.
By being there unconditionally for each other, a sense of belonging is created.

Three gaps: a particle expressing probability (not certainty), a formal connective adverb pushing against the previous clause, and an emphatic indefinite pronoun leaving no exception. Three distinct B2 registers in one sentence.
Count the tiny particles in each option. Natural German dinner conversation uses two layered particles that add shared recognition and warm emphasis.
Every single effort without exception. The most emphatic formal indefinite pronoun for totality.
Every single effort was worth it.
Apply the rule: adverbs of time come first, then reason, then manner, then place. Which option places each adverb in the correct slot?

Four gaps: the value of something, the subject of that value, the connector describing HOW understanding grows, and a particle signaling that the result will come. Consider what genuine bonds are worth and how they develop.
In a compound noun, the last element is always the boss of the gender. Identify which of the four options is actually the last element of this compound.
The formal verb meaning to gather or infer information from a text or document.
He could gather from the report that the exam had been passed.
The final lesson. Yilmaz's last words on the balcony. Berlin glows. The journey ends where home begins.
Complete the journey
Hear the final words of your mentor
Affirm that home is not a place but a feeling

The party winds down. The dishes are mostly cleared. The candles burn low. Some guests have left: KD first (early shift), then Lukas and Mia together, guitar slung over his back, her arm in his. Heinrich hugged everyone twice.
Hildi is the last to go. She looks around the apartment, then looks at Verumius. She says nothing. She squeezes his hand. Then she is gone.
Only Yilmaz remains. She holds a glass of Turkish tea. She stands at the window. Berlin glows.
Verumius. Come here a moment.
They stand at the window together. Teacher and student. The TV Tower blinks in the distance. The city hums.
Do you know what the hardest thing about learning a language is?
The grammar? The exam?
No. The hardest thing is being yourself. In another language. Most people learn words and rules. But they remain tourists in the language. You are no longer a tourist.
What am I then?
You are you. In German.
Silence. The city. The tea. Then Yilmaz picks up her coat, kisses him on both cheeks (Turkish tradition), and leaves. The door closes softly.
Verumius stands alone in his apartment. The candles flicker. The sourdough starter sits in its jar. The photos on the wall. The essay on the corkboard. Berlin outside the window.
I am home.
the taxpayer

A: Verumius is now a taxpayer.
B: Health insurance, tax office, everything.
A: A real member of society.
B: Integration on paper and in the heart.
from the bottom of one's heart

A: I thank you from the bottom of my heart.
B: That makes us very happy.
A: Without you I would not be here.
B: We are glad you are here.
heartfelt, sincerely

A: All the best, from the heart!
B: Thanks, that is kind.
A: I mean it sincerely.
B: I can feel it.
in the end, ultimately

A: In the end, only one thing matters.
B: What is that?
A: That you tried.
B: In the end, you are right.
finally, ultimately

A: In the end, everything was worth it.
B: Every single step?
A: Yes, ultimately even the mistakes.
B: Mistakes are the best teachers.
last but not least, finally

A: Last but not least, I want to say thank you.
B: For what?
A: For everything. Simply for everything.
B: That was the most beautiful closing word.
to dream

A: I dreamed of living here.
B: And now you live here.
A: The dream became reality.
B: That happens rarely. Enjoy it.
to be at peace, to be forgiving

A: Are you at peace with the past?
B: Yes, finally.
A: Even with the hard moments?
B: Especially with those. They shaped me.
looking back, in retrospect

A: Looking back, everything was right.
B: Every step?
A: Yes. In retrospect, even the detours.
B: Detours expand your knowledge of the area.
sincere, genuine

A: Sincere thanks for everything.
B: That sounds so serious.
A: It is seriously meant.
B: Then I gladly accept it.
at home

A: Are you finally home?
B: Yes. And it feels different than before.
A: Different how?
B: Like I belong here. I am home.
Yilmaz draws a contrast between performing a language and inhabiting it. The deepest compliment is about identity, not accuracy.
An adverb that positions the speaker at the present moment, casting their gaze backward over the entire journey. It frames reflection on the past from a place of arrival.
Looking back, everything was right. Every step. Every mistake. Every word.
Three closing gaps: a noun completing a fixed German phrase meaning 'ultimately,' the word for the place one calls home, and the emotion Verumius feels at the end of two years.
Zuhause is where you belong. Where the people are who know your name. Where you are yourself.
